methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing

data.narrative:
Nord Quantique is a seed-stage quantum computing company founded by Julien Camirand Lemyre and Philippe St-Jean that has raised over C$40M, including a C$23M round from Quantum Champions in December 2025. The quantum computing sector comprises 11 known comparables: 3 public companies (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private firms including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

Peer-median valuation analysis was not applicable to this engagement. The quantum computing space lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage peers at comparable valuation tiers—zero datapoints met the narrow-filter threshold of ≥3 comparable companies. While 7 same-sector peers exist, none satisfied simultaneous criteria for stage alignment and financial similarity. Falling back to all-sector median multiples would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity of quantum computing business models, technology maturity, and capital intensity across the peer universe.

The absence of reliable peer-based triangulation means Nord Quantique's valuation cannot be anchored to traded or recently-funded comparables using standard VC methodology. Any valuation range would require alternative approaches (venture capital method, DCF scenario analysis, or expert-elicitation frameworks) and would rest on assumptions about path-to-commercialization, hardware performance metrics, and market timing rather than observed market pricing. The thinness of the comparable set is itself a material input: it signals early-stage risk and the speculative nature of the sector.
